title: OTP Bypass
description: During security assessment, we have found out that the web application is using an outdated version of DOMPurify (v3.0.6). This version contains known security issues that may allow attackers to bypass sanitization, potentially leading to XSS attacks and exposing sensitive user data.
recommendation: To mitigate this issue, we recommend updating DOMPurify to the latest stable version and applying all relevant security patches. Ensure proper input sanitization practices are enforced and regularly monitor third-party libraries for vulnerabilities to prevent exploitation through outdated components.

title: No Input Validation
description: During security assessment, we have found out that the application does not properly validate numeric input fields. Users are able to enter alphabetic characters and excessively long values without restriction, which may lead to unexpected application behavior, data corruption, or potential injection attacks.
recommendation: To mitigate this issue, we recommend implementing strict input validation on all numeric fields. Enforce type checks, length restrictions, and allow only acceptable characters. Additionally, apply server-side validation to prevent bypassing client-side controls and ensure that invalid input is properly rejected.
